summary: The current branch is not ahead of main, so assessment used main/trunk mode. Visible issue scope is only partially implemented: the repo already has adjacent source-packet, requirement-status, TDD task, and issue-brief verification concepts, but it does not contain a moonspec-assess skill/command/export/projection, source acceptance matrix artifact protocol, orchestrate assessment step, or the resolved canonical design document docs/MoonSpecAcceptanceAssessment.md. No recent merge history for Jira MM-1135 was found; a GitHub PR #1135 merge exists but is unrelated by title. Trusted Jira recovery was unavailable in this runtime, so description and preset_brief retain residual truncation markers and hidden content was not assessed as a requirement.

requirements.description: Add a provider-neutral MoonSpec acceptance assessment workflow: source request -> moonspec-specify creates spec.md + source acceptance matrix -> moonspec-assess checks current repo against that matrix -> moonspec-plan / moonspec-tasks plan only missing or partial rows -> moonspec-implement consumes the bounded backlog -> moonspec-verify re-checks the same acceptance matrix before completion.
Keep preset inputs simple and do not add new optional inputs to moonspec-orchestrate.

Use a provider-neutral artifact protocol where the assessment skill accepts acceptance criteria from any source: moonspec-specify, a hand-authored matrix, a Jira brief, a GitHub issue brief, or a design document.
Avoid MoonMind coupling in MoonSpec artifacts and skill instructions, except as examples in tests.
Keep the source request controlling so generated spec.md, plan.md, and tasks.md do not silently narrow or override the original source acceptance criteria.
Assessment produces bounded backlog so implementation consumes missing or partial acceptance rows.
Provide the canonical source design path docs/MoonSpecAcceptanceAssessment.md resolved from the trusted issue brief.
